# Scientific Agent Skills (vendored)

This directory tracks the provenance of the Claude Code skills vendored from
**Scientific Agent Skills** by K-Dense Inc.:

- Source: <https://github.com/K-Dense-AI/scientific-agent-skills>
- Version vendored: v2.52.0 (commit `2093901`, 2026-06-15)
- License: MIT — see `LICENSE` in this directory
- 147 skills covering bioinformatics, cheminformatics, clinical research, medical
  imaging, machine learning, materials science, physics, geospatial science,
  laboratory automation, and scientific communication/writing

## What was vendored, and where

The entire upstream `skills/` directory (147 skill folders, each its own
`SKILL.md` plus optional `references/`, `scripts/`, `assets/`) was copied
as-is into `.claude/skills/`, matching the layout Claude Code already
auto-discovers project skills from.

## What was intentionally left out

Upstream development/CI/packaging surface that isn't needed to use the skills:

- `docs/` — generated catalog pages (`skills.md`, `examples.md`) and a 23MB
  promotional GIF; the categorized skill list in the main `README.md` (and
  upstream's own README) already covers this.
- `scan_skills.py`, `scan_pr_skills.py`, `pyproject.toml`, `CONTRIBUTING.md`,
  `.github/workflows/` — upstream's own skill-scanning/release/contribution
  tooling, not needed to run the skills themselves.

## A note on upstream's SECURITY.md

Upstream ships a `SECURITY.md` generated by an LLM-based static analyzer
(`cisco-ai-skill-scanner`) that flags 22 skills CRITICAL and 17 HIGH out of 147.
Before vendoring, every flagged skill's actual script files were manually read
and cross-checked against that report. The scanner turned out to be unreliable:
most CRITICAL/HIGH findings were either (a) fabricated entirely — claiming
specific Python files and exfiltration chains in directories that contain zero
scripts (e.g. `cellxgene-census`, `seaborn`, `tiledbvcf`, and most of the HIGH
list), or (b) a blanket "reads an env var and makes a network call" heuristic
applied to ordinary, documented API-key usage (e.g. a script reading
`OPENROUTER_API_KEY` and calling `openrouter.ai`). No skill exhibited real
malicious or privacy-invasive behavior — no shell injection, no eval/exec of
untrusted content, no unexpected network endpoints, no credential harvesting,
and no off-machine transmission of secrets — so all 147 skills were vendored.

Two minor (non-blocking) code-hygiene notes surfaced during review, left as-is
since they only affect the user's own local invocation, not a remote/untrusted
input path:

- `pacsomatic/scripts/run_pacsomatic.py` writes one CLI arg (`--module-load`)
  unescaped into a generated local shell script.
- `scientific-writing/scripts/generate_image.py` searches upward through parent
  directories for a `.env` file without a bound, though it only ever extracts
  `OPENROUTER_API_KEY`.

If you need any of the excluded upstream files, fetch them directly from the
source repository at the version noted above.
